TSLA.reasoning: Vote count: 3 bullish (mean_reversion 52, breakout 52, catalyst_news 58), 2 neutral (trend_momentum 30, macro_context 38). Agent agreement: 60% bullish. However, this is tepid bullishness — all three bullish agents cap their own confidence at 52-58, and every agent (including the bulls) flags the same core risk: the hourly chart is severely overextended right now (RSI-21 70.5, pct_b 1.27, price 5%+ above all hourly EMAs at current ~$396). This is a unified 'wait for the pullback' signal disguised as a 3-2 split. The bullish case rests on three convergent pillars: (1) Mean Reversion — daily close on 4/30 at $381.63 printed an intraday probe to $368 then closed near the 38.2% Fib ($381.76) with a bullish reversal candle; a bounce from Fib confluence with 14-test pivot support is technically sound. (2) Breakout — the hourly squeeze fired on 2.74x volume; BB width 0.0652 confirms prior coil broke cleanly; measured-move target $405.50. (3) Catalyst — post-earnings continuation window (7 days post-April 22), Musk stepping back from DOGE, robotaxi commercial operation in Austin (no competitor match), FSD Netherlands approval — net catalyst read is positive even with delivery gap concerns. The neutral agents are not wrong: trend_momentum correctly notes the daily EMA fan is not aligned (10/21 are below 50/200 EMA), the 10d ROC is negative, and no clean pullback-to-EMA entry exists yet. Macro_context correctly identifies that TSLA is underperforming a market at ATHs (21d +2.66% vs S&P best April since 2020) and that R/R at current $396 fails the 2:1 minimum — the first target at $409 is only 3.4% away while the stop at $378-381 is 4-5% below, yielding sub-1:1 R/R at the current price. Wiki dial-down applies: the $387-$399 zone is the same documented bear-rally resistance zone where the April 15 short was recommended at $391.86. The bulls are asking to enter INTO that same resistance cluster that previously created bearish setups. The NVDA EMA-Fib lesson (EMA pullback Fib dip-buy, 0% win rate, -$264.65) is directly analogous to today's setup type — stretched hourly RSI, no bullish confirmation candle before entry, overbought intraday conditions. This dials confidence down from what would otherwise be a cleaner 3-of-5 vote. Synthesized entry: $387 (cluster median of $385-$393 from the three bullish agents, adjusted to just above the upper boundary of the documented resistance zone base). Target: $407 (between measured-move $405.50 and swing-high $409.28; median of the range). Stop: $370 (median of the $363-$382.50 range across agents, set below the 50% Fib at $373.26 and near the cluster of bullish agent stops; gives the trade room to breathe through the resistance zone while not risking through the 61.8% Fib). At entry $387 / target $407 / stop $370: reward $20 vs risk $17 = 1.18:1 — still below the 2:1 minimum, confirming the macro agent's R/R math. This is the structural problem: no entry point between the current price and the resistance ceiling creates a 2:1 trade to the $409 target. The trade only reaches 2:1 if the target extends to the Fib extension at $428 — which requires breaking above 14-test resistance at $409, a step that current momentum (daily OBV still down, daily EMAs unaligned) does not yet support. Conclusion: directionally bullish but structurally constrained by resistance and R/R. The setup improves dramatically IF price pulls back to $381-385 (38.2% Fib / daily 21 EMA confluence) and posts a bullish daily candle — that entry with a $365 stop and $409 target yields 2.7:1. Until that pullback materializes, this is a watch, not an act.
TSLA.key_conflicts: The sharpest conflict is between swing_macro_context and the three bullish agents on R/R math. Macro correctly demonstrates that no entry point between current $396 and the $409 resistance ceiling clears the 2:1 minimum — the trade fails the asymmetry test at all plausible entries. The three bullish agents all propose LOWER entries ($385-393) precisely to solve this problem, but none of them have confirmed that the pullback to those levels will actually happen before the stock reverses. Swing_trend_momentum adds a second dimension: the daily EMA structure has not yet confirmed a trend reversal — short-term EMAs (10/21) are below the 50 and 200 EMA, and the 10d ROC is negative. The breakout and catalyst agents are essentially calling a trend reversal before the daily chart confirms it. The productive read of this disagreement: three agents see a directional catalyst (post-earnings, squeeze, Fib bounce) while two agents see structural incompleteness (no EMA alignment, R/R failure, TSLA underperforming ATH market). This is 'early in a potential recovery with insufficient confirmation' — not a coin flip, but not a clean setup either. The wiki's resistance zone documentation ($387-$399) is the tie-breaker: that zone killed the prior bearish thesis in April and is now the zone the bulls must clear convincingly (daily close above $399 on above-average volume) before trend_momentum and macro_context will flip to bullish. Until then, the breakout and catalyst signals are real but premature for a full-sized entry.
